variant_label: Macro / National Accounts — Real GNP growth rate
notes: Iran's economy grew 33% in 1973, described as the world's fastest-growing economy, sustained for over a decade. [cite: CIA/OER, "Iran: An Overview of the Shah's Economy" (S-6549), 16 October 1974, PDF p.2]

variant_label: Macro / National Accounts — Real GNP growth rate (expected)
notes: Growth expected to accelerate to 40% in 1974, driven by the recent oil-price surge. [cite: CIA/OER, "Iran: An Overview of the Shah's Economy" (S-6549), 16 October 1974, PDF p.2]

Iran's GNP of about $40 billion described as more than four times that of nearest Middle East rivals Egypt and Israel. [cite: CIA/OER, "Iran: An Overview of the Shah's Economy" (S-6549), 16 October 1974, PDF p.2]

variant_label: Government finance — Fifth Development Plan spending, originally planned
notes: Fifth Plan (1973-77) development spending originally planned at $23 billion before being expanded. [cite: CIA/OER, "Iran: An Overview of the Shah's Economy" (S-6549), 16 October 1974, PDF p.2]

variant_label: Government finance — Fifth Development Plan spending, expanded total
notes: Development spending expanded from the original $23 billion plan to $45 billion during the Fifth Plan. [cite: CIA/OER, "Iran: An Overview of the Shah's Economy" (S-6549), 16 October 1974, PDF p.2]

variant_label: Oil production & trade — Expected annual oil revenue, rest of decade
notes: The Shah could count on oil revenues of some $20 billion annually through the rest of the decade absent a sharp price reversal, to finance development plans. [cite: CIA/OER, "Iran: An Overview of the Shah's Economy" (S-6549), 16 October 1974, PDF p.2]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Iran's foreign lending/investment commitments
notes: In 1974 the Shah committed more than $8 billion to foreign lending and investment abroad (advances to France/UK, Krupp steel stake, aid credits to India, loans to Egypt/Syria/African nations) to recycle surplus oil funds. [cite: CIA/OER, "Iran: An Overview of the Shah's Economy" (S-6549), 16 October 1974, PDF p.2]

About $8 billion spent on armaments in 1967-73, the largest arms buildup in the Middle East, aimed at Persian Gulf dominance and Indian Ocean influence. [cite: CIA/OER, "Iran: An Overview of the Shah's Economy" (S-6549), 16 October 1974, PDF p.3]

variant_label: Military expenditure — US F-14/Phoenix missile contract value
notes: Contract signed for $600 million for US F-14 fighters armed with Phoenix air-to-air missiles. [cite: CIA/OER, "Iran: An Overview of the Shah's Economy" (S-6549), 16 October 1974, PDF p.3]
Price increase of 6% in 1972, cited as a baseline before the 1973-74 acceleration. [cite: CIA/OER, "Iran: An Overview of the Shah's Economy" (S-6549), 16 October 1974, PDF p.3]
Price increase of 11% in 1973. [cite: CIA/OER, "Iran: An Overview of the Shah's Economy" (S-6549), 16 October 1974, PDF p.3]

variant_label: Prices — Consumer/wholesale price inflation, annualized rate
notes: Prices during the first quarter of 1974 ran at an annual rate of 16%, attributed to large government outlays and enormous capital inflow. [cite: CIA/OER, "Iran: An Overview of the Shah's Economy" (S-6549), 16 October 1974, PDF p.3]

variant_label: Inequality & Wealth — Income share of top 20 percent
notes: Despite large government spending, the top 20% of the population probably still received over half of total income. [cite: CIA/OER, "Iran: An Overview of the Shah's Economy" (S-6549), 16 October 1974, PDF p.3]

variant_label: Inequality & Wealth — Income share of bottom 20 percent
notes: The lowest 20% of the population received only about 5% of total income -- paired with the top-20% figure to show persistent inequality despite the oil boom. [cite: CIA/OER, "Iran: An Overview of the Shah's Economy" (S-6549), 16 October 1974, PDF p.3]

variant_label: Government finance — Planned investment in industrial expansion
notes: Tehran plans to invest $6 billion in industrial expansion during the current (Fifth) Plan, an opportunity area for US business (design/equipment/installation). [cite: CIA/OER, "Iran: An Overview of the Shah's Economy" (S-6549), 16 October 1974, PDF p.3]

variant_label: Government finance — Planned investment in oil and gas development
notes: Tehran plans to invest $5 billion in oil and gas development during the current Plan. [cite: CIA/OER, "Iran: An Overview of the Shah's Economy" (S-6549), 16 October 1974, PDF p.3]

variant_label: Trade — Iranian imports of goods and services incl. arms, expected annual level
notes: Iranian imports of goods and services, including arms, expected to swell to about $10 billion annually by 1975. [cite: CIA/OER, "Iran: An Overview of the Shah's Economy" (S-6549), 16 October 1974, PDF p.3]

variant_label: Trade — Resulting US sales to Iran
notes: The $10 billion import level was expected to result in US sales of at least $1.6 billion, with the US needing to counter growing Japanese/West European competition to retain share. [cite: CIA/OER, "Iran: An Overview of the Shah's Economy" (S-6549), 16 October 1974, PDF p.3]
